description: Redis password generation drift caused by Helm include re-evaluation producing different random passwords across templates
root_cause: Helm's {{ include }} directive re-evaluates the named template on every call. When the redis.password helper generates a random password (no explicit password set), each {{ include "redis.password" }} invocation produces a DIFFERENT random value. This means templates/secret.yaml, templates/health-configmap.yaml, and the ServiceBinding resource all get different passwords.

drift_points.actual: Random password A (generated on first include call)
override_chain: templates/_helpers.tpl: redis.password helper generates random value

drift_points.actual: Random password B (different from Secret due to re-evaluation)
override_chain: templates/_helpers.tpl: redis.password helper generates NEW random value
templates/_helpers.tpl: redis.password helper generates NEW random value

fix_approach: Store the generated password in .Values.global.redis.password on first call so subsequent include calls reuse it instead of regenerating
